options: Every action has equal and opposite reaction
A body in rest stays at rest
explanation: Newton's Second Law: \( \vec{F}_{net} = m\vec{a} \). More precisely: \( \vec{F} = \frac{d\vec{p}}{dt} \) (rate of change of momentum).
name: Newton's Second Law

explanation: Impulse \( J = F\Delta t = \Delta p = mv - mu \). Explains why airbags (increase \(\Delta t\)) reduce force in collisions.
name: Impulse-Momentum Theorem

text: A block of mass 5 kg is on a frictionless surface. A force of 20 N is applied. Its acceleration is:
name: Newton's 2nd Law Calculation

text: The coefficient of static friction \(\mu_s\) and kinetic friction \(\mu_k\) satisfy:
explanation: Static friction > kinetic friction: \( \mu_s > \mu_k \). It is harder to start sliding than to keep sliding.
name: Static vs Kinetic Friction

text: Law of conservation of linear momentum holds when:
options: Net external force on the system is zero
All particles move with same velocity
explanation: Linear momentum is conserved when net external force = 0: \( \frac{d\vec{p}}{dt} = \vec{F}_{ext} = 0 \Rightarrow \vec{p} = \text{const} \).
name: Conservation of Momentum

text: A rocket works on the principle of:
explanation: Rocket propulsion: burning gases ejected backward (action) → rocket moves forward (reaction). Based on Newton's 3rd law and conservation of momentum.
name: Rocket Propulsion

text: The maximum angle of a rough inclined plane at which a body just begins to slide is called:
explanation: Angle of repose \( \theta_r \): \( \tan\theta_r = \mu_s \). The block just starts sliding when inclination reaches \( \theta_r \).
name: Angle of Repose

text: For a body on a rough horizontal surface, the limiting friction is:
explanation: Limiting static friction \( f_{s,max} = \mu_s N \). For any applied force \( F \leq f_{s,max} \): body remains static.
name: Limiting Friction

text: In a head-on elastic collision between equal masses where one is at rest, after collision:
options: First stops, second moves with first's velocity
explanation: Equal mass elastic collision: first body stops, second moves with velocity of first. Velocities exchange completely.
name: Elastic Collision Equal Masses

text: The apparent weight of a person in a lift accelerating upward is:
explanation: Apparent weight: \( W' = m(g+a) \) when accelerating up (heavier feeling). \( W' = m(g-a) \) when accelerating down.
name: Apparent Weight in Lift

text: A gun recoils when fired. This is explained by:
explanation: Before firing: total momentum = 0. After: bullet moves forward, gun recoils backward. Total momentum still = 0. Conservation of momentum.
name: Gun Recoil

text: Rolling friction is _____ than sliding friction:
explanation: Rolling friction \( \ll \) sliding friction. That's why wheels (ball bearings) reduce friction significantly. \( \mu_{rolling} \ll \mu_{sliding} \).
name: Rolling vs Sliding Friction

text: For a conical pendulum, the tension in the string provides:
options: Both vertical support and centripetal force (horizontal component provides centripetal)
explanation: Conical pendulum: \( T\cos\theta = mg \) (vertical), \( T\sin\theta = \frac{mv^2}{r} \) (centripetal horizontal).
name: Conical Pendulum

text: The pseudo force (fictitious force) appears in:
explanation: Pseudo force: \( \vec{F}_{pseudo} = -m\vec{a}_{frame} \). Appears in non-inertial frames to explain motion using Newton's laws.
name: Pseudo Force

text: The banking of roads is done to provide:
options: Centripetal force without depending on friction
explanation: Banking: \( \tan\theta = \frac{v^2}{rg} \). Normal force component provides centripetal force without relying on friction. Optimal speed: \( v = \sqrt{rg\tan\theta} \).
name: Banking of Roads
